title: Spotify callback lacks failure handling for the outbound token exchange
reasoning: The route does not wrap the `fetch` call or `tokenRes.json()` parsing in a `try`/`catch`. Network failures, DNS issues, or a non-JSON upstream response will throw and bypass the route's JSON error shape entirely, producing an unstructured 500 instead of the documented JSON `{ error: ... }` responses used elsewhere in the handler.
recommendation: Wrap the token exchange and JSON parsing in `try`/`catch`, return a stable JSON error body on transport/parse failures, and consider propagating the upstream status when Spotify returns a structured error.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: There are no route tests exercising transport failures or malformed upstream responses, so the uncaught-throw path is currently unverified.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock `fetch` to throw and to return invalid JSON, then assert the route still responds with JSON and an explicit error status/body.
